question_text: A tennis ball is rolled across three different surfaces: carpet, tile and polished wood. Which surface would result in the least friction, and why?

options.label: A
options.text: Carpet, because it is the thickest material

options.label: B
options.text: Polished wood, because it has the smoothest surface

options.label: C
options.text: Tile, because it is the hardest material

options.label: D
options.text: Carpet, because it absorbs the ball's energy

correct_answer: B) Polished wood, because it has the smoothest surface
explanation: Friction increases with surface roughness. Polished wood is the smoothest surface, so it produces the least friction. Carpet is rougher, and while tile is hard, it is still textured enough to create more friction than polished wood.
